hero.imageAlt: Mountain panorama at sunset with hikers
hero.title: Activities
hero.subtitle: There is always something to do. And always space to do nothing at all.

intro:
Life on Sømliøya happens mostly outside.

The island and the surrounding fjord offer space for simple things: exploring the landscape, spending time by the water or gathering around a fire. There is always something to do.  And of course always space to do nothing at all.

items.imageAlt: View across the fjord with a small boat
items.title: Kayaking
items.description: The fjord is calm and sheltered in many places, making it possible to explore the coast and quiet bays by kayak.

items.imageAlt: Fishing rods against the fjord with mountain backdrop
items.title: Fishing
items.description: Fishing has always been part of life along the fjord. Cod, pollock and mackerel are common in these waters.

items.imageAlt: Person fishing from rocks in Norwegian sweater
items.title: Swimming
items.description: The sea is cold and clear. You can jump from the rocks and warm up afterwards by the fire or in the house.

items.imageAlt: Two hikers at mountain summit at sunset
items.title: Walking & Hiking
items.description: The island and nearby areas offer simple coastal walks across rocks, forest paths and open shoreline.

items.imageAlt: Silhouette of hiker against dramatic orange sunset
items.title: Heilhornet
items.description: One of the most distinctive peaks in the region. A day trip to the summit offers wide views across fjords, islands and the open Norwegian Sea.

items.imageAlt: Hikers on rocky mountain summit ridge
items.title: Wildlife Watching
items.description: Sea eagles are often seen above the fjord, and roe deer and moose move through nearby forests.

regionSection.heading: Exploring the Region

places.name: Island of Leka
places.description:
Leka is a small island off the coast, known for its distinctive red and orange rock formations—some of the oldest geological formations in the country. The landscape is surprisingly varied, with rugged mountains, open coastline, quiet beaches, and wide views over the sea.

It’s also home to what is often considered one of the most beautiful hikes in Norway, with trails that take you through this unique geology and out to panoramic coastal views.

regionSection.imageAlt: Two people at summit cairn overlooking fjords at sunset

fireSection.heading: Around the Fire
fireSection.closingImageAlt: Group of people dining at outdoor table by the fjord
